headline: Australia’s Queensland commits AU$3.2 billion to CopperString transmission project
summary_80w: Australia’s Queensland has allocated AU$3.2 billion to the CopperString transmission project in its 2026-27 State Budget.

headline: SNEC 2026: BESS overtakes PV modules in expo floor space, more than 97GWh of deals signed
summary_80w: Carrie Xiao reports back from this year's edition of SNEC in Shanghai, China, the world's biggest solar and energy storage expo.

headline: DNV verifies 98.7% availability of Fluence’s global battery energy storage fleet
summary_80w: DNV has independently verified that Fluence's global fleet of battery energy storage systems (BESS) achieved 98.7% MW-weighted availability.

headline: Gamuda Renewables acquires interest in 450MW solar-plus-storage site in Victoria, Australia
summary_80w: Gamuda Renewables has secured an interest in the 450MW Hazelwood North solar-plus-storage project from Latrobe Valley-based developer Manthos Investments.

headline: Brookfield, Mitsubishi launch US$462 million European renewables platform
summary_80w: Brookfield and Mitsubishi HC Capital have formed a JV anchored by a 570MW European portfolio valued at approximately US$462 million.

headline: Lodestone Energy to begin construction on 31.5MWdc solar PV plant in New Zealand
summary_80w: Lodestone Energy & Centralines have confirmed that construction will begin this spring on a NZ$50 million solar PV power plant in New Zealand.

headline: JA Solar rebrands to JA in evolution to ‘green energy partner’
summary_80w: JA has dropped ‘solar’ from its name to reflect its shift from PV manufacturing to a wider clean energy technology and services brief.

headline: Trinasolar tandem module reaches record 907W power output

headline: US solar and storage defy political hostility to dominate Q1 power installations
summary_80w: New figures from SEIA and Wood Mackenzie reveal that solar and storage accounted for 91% of new additions to the US grid in Q1 2026.

headline: Qcells begins solar cell production at 3.3GW vertically integrated US facility
summary_80w: Solar manufacturer Qcells has started producing solar cells at its vertically integrated manufacturing facility in Cartersville, Georgia.

headline: How solar-plus-storage can answer Europe’s renewable energy questions
summary_80w: Despite technical challenges, co-locating solar PV and BESS could provide an answer to many of Europe's renewable energy challenges.

headline: IEEFA identifies ‘missing middle’ as Australian businesses install fraction of rooftop solar potential
summary_80w: Australia leads the world in residential rooftop solar, but its commercial and industrial sector has deployed only 5.6GW.

headline: Challenges remain for inertia in Germany, but value to BESS ‘will only go up’

headline: US ROUNDUP: EDP 800MWh Arizona project, Pathway targets SPP & MISO, Waymo second-life partnership

summary_80w: Minimising counterparty risk is a key component of the German BESS sector, particularly amid ongoing political uncertainty.

headline: Beyond modules: why US solar’s future depends on upstream manufacturing scale-up

With 66GW of module capacity chasing just 11GW of domestic cells, the supply chain crunch is reaching a critical inflection point, write Moustafa Ramadan and Joe Hennessy.
